moment_text: Well, I love the question and I think that mental wellbeing is really the journey of every woman, whether we consciously work towards it or whether we don't. And so what that does is it creates a contrast between yourself and the world because a lot of the time we almost pride ourselves on indifference or we pride ourselves on perhaps more of an emotionless approach. moment_text: So the first time that I really became aware of. That I didn't, I didn't really, I don't really think it matters so much because I think that any person, any child at any age when they experience something so potent or when they experience something that is so. Life altering or perhaps life shattering, even if they don't necessarily accept it or consider it intentionally, it becomes very much a part of their foundation, a part of their personality. Potent shattering, terrifying, but also wildly curious experiences that were going on around me from a very, very young age. A massive part of our lives, and it's only later that we realize that we've actually been on a personal mission from a very, very young age.
